steps.title: Identify the context for punctuation
steps.content: The phrase "find the answer" signals that a question was posed. The blank must contain a question.

steps.title: Apply question punctuation
steps.content:
"Find the answer" signals a question was posed. A direct question needs inverted word order (auxiliary before subject) AND a question mark.
  • Choice A — declarative + period: "If elevated temperatures continue, these penguins could become extinct." — statement word order, period; not a question at all. ✗
  • Choice B — declarative + question mark: "If elevated temperatures continue, these penguins could become extinct?" — question mark is present but "these penguins could" keeps the subject before the auxiliary; still sounds declarative. ✗
  • Choice C — inverted + period: "If elevated temperatures continue, could these penguins become extinct." — inverted order is correct, but the period fails to mark it as a question. ✗
  • Choice D — inverted + question mark: "If elevated temperatures continue, could these penguins become extinct?" — auxiliary "could" comes before subject "these penguins"; question mark closes correctly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
